briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting risks in the embedded banking model.

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — Over 130 million Europeans are transitioning to a sovereign payment system, reducing reliance on Visa and Mastercard networks.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is transforming the traditional credit card model with increasingly niche and unconventional product offerings.

- **[Consumer Comfort with AI for Severe Weather Monitoring Climbs to 51% in 2026, Insurity Survey Finds](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-wire-news-releases-pmn/consumer-comfort-with-ai-for-severe-weather-monitoring-climbs-to-51-in-2026-insurity-survey-finds)** — *Financial Post* — A new Insurity report reveals that more than half of consumers are now comfortable with AI-driven risk monitoring for severe weather, signalling growing acceptance of AI in insurtech.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Relay secures $50 million USD to bring more small businesses to its banking platform](https://betakit.com/relay-secures-50m-usd-to-bring-more-small-businesses-to-its-banking-platform/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian fintech Relay has raised $50 million USD to expand its small business banking platform and serve more entrepreneurs across North America.

- **[Lightspeed beats revenue projections but falls short on earnings expectations in fiscal Q4](https://betakit.com/lightspeed-beats-revenue-projections-but-falls-short-on-earnings-expectations-in-fiscal-q4/)** — *BetaKit* — Montreal-based payments and commerce platform Lightspeed exceeded revenue forecasts in Q4 but missed earnings expectations, reflecting ongoing profitability pressures.

- **[Sagard spins up new $150-million USD AI fund](https://betakit.com/sagard-spins-up-new-150-million-usd-ai-fund/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian alternative asset manager Sagard has launched a $150 million USD fund dedicated to AI investments, signalling growing institutional appetite for AI-driven ventures.

- **[N49P's $25-million USD first close for Fund IV will help it hunt more "diamonds in the rough"](https://betakit.com/n49ps-25-million-usd-first-close-for-fund-iv-will-help-it-hunt-more-diamonds-in-the-rough/)** — *BetaKit* — Vancouver-based early-stage VC N49P has completed a $25 million USD first close for its fourth fund, targeting underdog startups across Canada.

- **[Digital surveillance Bill C-22 threatens to drive tech firms out of Canada](https://betakit.com/digital-surveillance-bill-c-22-threatens-to-drive-tech-firms-out-of-canada/)** — *BetaKit* — Industry voices warn that proposed digital surveillance legislation Bill C-22 could push technology and fintech companies to relocate outside of Canada.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and committed to an open banking framework, a regulatory foundation still shaping Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintechs today.

- **[Historic Marconi name returns as Canadian investors launch defence tech firm](https://betakit.com/historic-marconi-name-returns-as-canadian-investors-launch-defence-prime/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian investors have revived the iconic Marconi brand to launch a new defence technology firm — notable for Atlantic Canada given Marconi's deep historic ties to Newfoundland and Nova Scotia.

- **[What tech CEOs want from the new federal AI strategy](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bakx-ai-strategy-federal-tech-data-9.7206174?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canadian tech leaders are calling on the federal government to develop a clear national AI strategy, with implications for fintech innovation and digital infrastructure investment across Atlantic Canada.
